coverage: 100% of reachable node types; the browser evaluates all 1,007 nodes in the root closure.
validation: Four Blender/VM cases cover authored settings, wrapper switching, thickness, and wrapper dimensions. Blender-float Newell face normals and safe-acos corner weights recover 38 authored vertices and make the authored face count exact: 61,697 / 53,892 versus Blender's 61,812 / 53,892, with bounds within 0.0303 units. Thickness 6 is 41,857 / 33,417 versus 42,031 / 33,434, but one outer Y extent remains 0.9862 units short; the 60 by 45 wrapper is 38,240 / 33,680 versus 38,301 / 33,678 and within 0.0100 units. Corrected multi-output nested probes establish the upstream boundary directly: shrink receives a bit-exact 57,757 / 57,276 mesh, and its first Raycast-driven Set Position introduces the full position residual. Raycast selects exactly the same 52,555 hits as Blender. Its Object Info target is topology-exact at 129,116 / 126,833 but differs from the first vertex onward, with 0.0000453 maximum error and 0.0000239 RMS error; the near-tangent projection amplifies this to a 0.006813 hit-position delta. The extractor/runtime now support exact evaluated float32 matrices plus active-object-specific relative dependency matrices. In a temporary re-extraction this reduces target maximum error to 0.0000153 and RMS error to 0.00000690, but the final authored mesh becomes 61,680 / 53,891 instead of the current 61,697 / 53,892. The public dump is therefore unchanged: exact matrices are an opt-in portable capability, while Grill still needs downstream float/order reconciliation before adopting them. Weld, proximity, autosmooth, normal tolerances, and epsilon snapping remain ruled out.
